title: Character Identity & Status — Best-in-Market Pixel Art Identity System
description: Transform character identity from basic monospace text pills and colored dots into a best-in-market pixel-art identity system. Bitmap font atlas, brand-colored nameplates, animated status icons, task context text, and personality idle animations.

in: 5x7 pixel bitmap font atlas through spriteCache pipeline
Brand-colored identity plates with role tags for all agents
Animated status icon sprites (gear, coffee, warning, hourglass)
Zoom-responsive rendering (sprite icons >= zoom 2, colored dots below, size cap at zoom 6+)
Personality idle animations (C-suite unique, others generic)
Dead code cleanup (old NAME_LABEL_*, STATUS_DOT_*, STATUS_COLOR_* constants)

dod.criterion: 5x7 bitmap font atlas with full alphanumeric charset, pixel-crisp at all zoom levels
Two-tier cached bitmap text renderer (no per-frame GC churn)
Brand-colored identity plates with role tags for all 11 agents + CEO
Animated sprite status icons for all 5 statuses with zoom-responsive rendering
Error status flash demands attention more than other statuses
Task context text below nameplate for working/waiting agents
Personality idle animations for all 11 agents (C-suite unique, others generic)
Old renderNameLabels(), renderStatusIndicators(), and all dead constants removed
VISUAL GATE: CEO scans office and identifies every agent's name, status, and task within 3 seconds

tasks.title: Define 5x7 Pixel Glyph Patterns
tasks.scope: Create sprites/bitmapFont.ts with 5x7 pixel glyph patterns for A-Z, a-z, 0-9, and punctuation. Define as compact string templates, expand to SpriteData at init. Fixed-width 5 columns, 1px spacing. Hidden at zoom < 2.
tasks.user_scenario: The bitmap font has crisp, legible pixel-art glyphs for all alphanumeric characters that look native to the game's art style.
tasks.verify: npm run type-check && npm run build

dod.criterion: bitmapFont.ts exports glyph SpriteData for A-Z, a-z, 0-9, and punctuation set
Each glyph is exactly 5 wide x 7 tall pixels
Glyph data defined as compact string templates, expanded to SpriteData at init
All characters legible at zoom 3 — verified by human in browser screenshot
Browser screenshot at zoom 3 and zoom 5 confirms glyphs are pixel-crisp with no anti-aliasing

tasks.title: Bitmap Text Renderer with Two-Tier Cache
tasks.scope: Build renderBitmapText() with two-tier cache: Map<string, SpriteData> for text composition + WeakMap for zoom scaling. Compose glyphs horizontally. Hidden at zoom < 2.
tasks.user_scenario: Agent names render as crisp pixel text that scales perfectly at any zoom level with no visible flicker, jank, or performance issues during zoom changes.
tasks.verify: npm run type-check && npm run build

dod.criterion: renderBitmapText() renders arbitrary strings in any hex color at any zoom level
Two-tier cache: Map<string, SpriteData> for text composition + WeakMap for zoom scaling
Text renders without visible flicker or jank during zoom changes
Text hidden at zoom < 2
Browser screenshot confirms crisp rendering at zoom 2, 3, and 5

tasks.title: Extend IdentityOverlay & Wire Agent Data
tasks.scope: Extend IdentityOverlay with colorMap, roleMap. Add colorNameToHex lookup. Wire in CanvasOffice.tsx. CEO = name 'You', color gold, role 'CEO'. Agents with no status default to offline.
tasks.user_scenario: All identity data flows correctly from agent-registry.json through to the renderer for every agent including the CEO player character.
tasks.verify: npm run type-check && npm run build

dod.criterion: IdentityOverlay extended with colorMap and roleMap
colorNameToHex mapping covers all 11 agents — all visually distinguishable
CanvasOffice.tsx populates colorMap and roleMap from OFFICE_AGENTS
CEO player character resolves to name='You', color=gold, role='CEO'
Agents with no status data default to offline
All 11 agents + CEO resolve to correct brand color and role

tasks.title: Brand-Colored Identity Plates with Role Tags
tasks.scope: Replace renderNameLabels() with renderIdentityPlates(). Brand-colored plates, bitmap font, role tags. Cache by agent ID. Remove old constants. Handle nameplate collision with opacity falloff. Maintain speech bubble z-order.
tasks.user_scenario: Each agent's nameplate is immediately recognizable by their brand color. Role abbreviation visible. CEO can identify who's who at a glance.
tasks.verify: npm run type-check && npm run build

dod.criterion: renderIdentityPlates() replaces renderNameLabels() using bitmap font
Each agent nameplate shows brand color prominently with role tag
Old renderNameLabels() and NAME_LABEL_* constants fully removed
Plate backgrounds cached by agent ID — no per-frame allocation
Speech bubbles render cleanly above identity plates
Overlapping nameplates handled with opacity falloff
Sitting offset and matrixEffect skip preserved
VISUAL GATE: Browser screenshot of all agents with nameplates approved

tasks.title: Status Icon Sprite Data (8x8, Multi-Frame)
tasks.scope: Create sprites/statusIcons.ts with 8x8 SpriteData for 5 statuses. Error gets 4 aggressive flash frames for attention-grab.
tasks.user_scenario: Five distinct, recognizable pixel-art status icons exist as sprite data. Each recognizable at zoom 3 without a legend.
tasks.verify: npm run type-check && npm run build

dod.criterion: statusIcons.ts exports SpriteData arrays for all 5 statuses
Working: 4 frames, Idle: 2 frames, Error: 4 frames (aggressive flash), Waiting: 2 frames, Offline: 1 frame
All frames are exactly 8x8 pixels
Each icon recognizable at zoom 3 without a legend — verified by human
Browser screenshot at zoom 3 and zoom 6 confirms visual quality

tasks.title: Replace Status Dots with Sprite-Based Icons
tasks.scope: Replace renderStatusIndicators() with renderStatusIcons(). Animate via identity.time. Zoom < 2: colored dots fallback. Zoom >= 6: size cap. Remove old constants.
tasks.user_scenario: CEO sees spinning gear (working), coffee cup (idle), flashing warning (error), hourglass (waiting). Status readable from animation alone.
tasks.verify: npm run type-check && npm run build

dod.criterion: renderStatusIcons() replaces renderStatusIndicators() with sprite-based rendering
Animation cycles correctly based on identity.time, synced with game loop
Error status flash is visually prominent — demands attention more than other statuses
At zoom < 2: falls back to simple colored dots for ambient awareness
At zoom >= 6: icon size capped so it does not dominate character sprite
Old renderStatusIndicators() and STATUS_DOT_*/STATUS_COLOR_* constants fully removed
Offline status hidden, matrixEffect skip guard preserved
Browser screenshot confirms all 5 statuses at zoom 1 (dots), zoom 3 (icons), and zoom 6 (capped)

tasks.title: Task Context Text Below Nameplate
tasks.scope: Add taskTextMap to IdentityOverlay. Bitmap text below nameplate, 50% alpha, truncate ~20 chars, working/waiting only, hidden zoom < 2. Null-safe.
tasks.user_scenario: Below Sarah's nameplate, the CEO sees 'reviewing auth fix' — instantly knowing what she's working on without clicking.
tasks.verify: npm run type-check && npm run build

dod.criterion: taskTextMap added to IdentityOverlay, wired from CanvasOffice.tsx
Task context renders below nameplate in dimmer bitmap text
Text truncated to ~20 chars with bitmap ellipsis
Only shown for working/waiting agents, hidden for idle/offline/error
Hidden at zoom < 2
Null/empty task text handled gracefully
VISUAL GATE: CEO scans office and identifies every agent's name, status, and task within 3 seconds

tasks.title: Personality Idle Sprite Frames
tasks.scope: Create 16x24 personality idle sprite frames for all 11 agents. C-suite unique, others role-themed generics. Add personality_idle to CharacterSprites.
tasks.user_scenario: Each agent has visually distinct personality idle frames — Sarah's glasses adjustment looks different from Morgan's clipboard check.
tasks.verify: npm run type-check && npm run build

dod.criterion: Personality idle sprite frames defined for all 11 agents
C-suite animations are visually unique (not palette-swapped)
Non-C-suite animations are role-themed generics
CharacterSprites interface extended with personality_idle
All frames match existing 16x24 character sprite dimensions
Browser screenshot confirms each C-suite idle is visually distinct at zoom 3

tasks.title: Personality Idle Trigger Logic
tasks.scope: Sprite override within IDLE state. personalityTimer + personalityFrame on Character. 8-15s random threshold. Plays once, returns to idle. No trigger for player CEO.
tasks.user_scenario: While watching the office, the CEO notices agents occasionally doing personality-specific actions while standing idle — subtle life that makes each character feel real.
tasks.verify: npm run type-check && npm run build

dod.criterion: personalityTimer and personalityFrame added to Character interface
Personality animation triggers after random 8-15s idle threshold
Uses sprite override in getCharacterSprite() — no new CharacterState added
Animation plays once then returns to static idle (no stuck states)
Only triggers in IDLE state, never interrupts WALK or TYPE
Does not trigger for isPlayerControlled characters
VISUAL GATE: Browser recording of at least 3 agents playing distinct personality idles
